Diesel cracks at historical highs across all regions.

The US rejects Iran charging tolls or fees to cross Hormuz. 

Meantime, also the US ⤵️ https://t.co/gshFhi5Qd0

COLUMN: The end of the US-Iran war has brought oil down to ~$75 a barrel. But the escalation of the Russia-Ukraine war is inflating the cost of petroleum refined products. The result? Fuel prices remain as high as if crude was still above $100. 

@Opinion https://t.co/DMRh8JH6xF

GLOBAL REFINING margins are nearing record highs following the breakdown of the ceasefire between the United States and Iran, and Ukraine’s attacks on Russia’s oil refineries. In the United States, the crack spread for turning three barrels of WTI crude into two barrels of gasoline and one barrel of diesel has averaged $61 per barrel so far in July. After adjusting for inflation, the benchmark 3-2-1 crack is the second-highest on record after $64 in June 2022, shortly after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the imposition of sanctions by the United States and Europe in response:
This oil market is so far from normal, words cannot describe what I’m seeing.
In a normal oil market, if Kharg Island even got a whiff of being attacked, +$15/bbl.

Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: war on.

No visible inbound tankers in the last 24 hours.

Reports stated that Iran fired at two vessels today. There was an escort that saw tankers exit, ATARAKTOS, was one of the VLCCs that exited.

With no diplomatic resolution for the Oman lane, we will likely see more escalation throughout this week. 

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker list.

Production shut-in excluding Iran is back to -7.5 million b/d. 

If the inbound tanker count remains low, we will be back to -9 million b/d by the end of the month.

Iran said Monday it would not agree to a resumption of international inspections at some of the country's nuclear facilities.

In response to a question on whether Iran would accept theUnited Nations' International Atomic Energy Agency's (IAEA) request to access nuclear facilities, Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei said the regime would not. https://t.co/ZVLLuj4H9Y

See How Ukraine Is Taking Out Russia’s Refineries
Long-range strikes unleash fuel crisis in Russia and win rare praise for Ukraine from Trump
#oott https://t.co/JQliHf7mGN

Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than Two Decades
Crude-processing rates have averaged 3.91 million barrels a day so far this month, the lowest level since March 2005, according to figures compiled by EA Analytics. That’s more than 1.4 million barrels a day below the year-ago average, the data show.
#oott https://t.co/b3BBTFCkl0
Trump says the US should control the Strait of Hormuz and get paid for it https://t.co/ETWTvNcMqo

Transits through the Strait of Hormuz have unsurprisingly collapsed following the ramp in attacks.

Yesterday's few transits were all dark, and while those numbers will inch higher as more dark transits are discovered and vetted we're undoubtedly heading in the wrong direction. https://t.co/17m15CbyoJ

Russia's seaborne oil product exports dropped by 27% on a daily basis in June month-on-month and by 37% from the same month a year ago to 5.7 million metric tons, as relentless drone attacks forced unplanned shutdowns at key domestic refineries, according to industry sources and Reuters calculations. - Reuters
